breaking_news.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Triggers Self-Spreading CanisterWorm Across 47 npm Packages
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors compromised the Trivy vulnerability scanner, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases and GitHub Actions used by thousands of CI/CD workflows. This supply chain attack triggered a self-spreading worm dubbed CanisterWorm across 47 npm packages, leveraging ICP canisters for propagation.

The breach forces organizations to treat all pipeline secrets as compromised and rotate them immediately to prevent a cascade of additional supply-chain compromises. Security firms traced the root cause to incomplete credential rotation after an earlier breach, allowing attackers to return and introduce malicious commits.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Used by State-Sponsored Hackers and Spyware Vendors
breaking_news.summary:
A new iOS exploit kit named DarkSword is being used by multiple threat actors to steal sensitive data from Apple devices via drive-by attacks. The toolkit leverages six vulnerabilities, including three zero-days, to achieve full device compromise without user interaction.

State-sponsored actors and commercial surveillance vendors have deployed this chain against targets in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine since late 2025. Apple has urged users to update iOS immediately to mitigate risks from malicious web content triggering infection chains.

breaking_news.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco Firewall Zero-Day Weeks Before Patch
breaking_news.summary:
The Interlock ransomware gang exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software weeks before a patch was released. Amazon security researchers discovered the group abused CVE-2026-20131, a maximum-severity deserialization flaw, to gain root access since late January.

This active exploitation turns a standard urgent patch into a full-blown zero-day emergency for organizations relying on Cisco firewalls for network security. CISA has subsequently ordered federal agencies to patch the flaw by Sunday, highlighting the severity of the unauthenticated remote code execution risk.

breaking_news.title: Handala Hacktivists Wipe Stryker Systems Using Microsoft Intune Abuse
breaking_news.summary:
Pro-Iranian threat actor Handala conducted a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker, wiping approximately 80,000 devices. The attackers bypassed traditional malware by compromising Microsoft Intune, a legitimate endpoint management system, to execute the wipe.

CISA and the FBI have warned organizations to harden their Intune configurations following the incident, emphasizing phishing-resistant authentication. This attack demonstrates a shift towards abusing trusted infrastructure to disrupt critical medical supply chains and operations.

breaking_news.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers disclosed a critical unpatched vulnerability in GNU InetUtils telnetd that enables unauthenticated remote code execution as root. Tracked as CVE-2026-32746 with a CVSS score of 9.8, the flaw stems from a buffer overflow in the LINEMODE handler affecting legacy infrastructure.

Because the vulnerability allows attackers to take full control before authentication, it poses a severe risk to networking equipment and embedded systems still running Telnet. A patch is expected by April 1, 2026, but users are urged to disable the service immediately to prevent exploitation.

breaking_news.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record DDoS Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Department of Justice disrupted command-and-control infrastructure for four major IoT botnets responsible for record-breaking DDoS attacks. The operation targeted Aisuru, KimWolf, JackSkid, and Mossad, which compromised over three million devices including routers and web cameras.

Authorities from Canada and Germany joined the effort to weaken global cybercrime activities that overloaded websites and defense systems with traffic bursts topping 30 terabits per second. This takedown significantly reduces the capacity for large-scale distributed denial-of-service campaigns targeting critical infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Critical Langflow Flaw Exploited Within 20 Hours of Disclosure
breaking_news.summary:
A critical security flaw in Langflow, tracked as CVE-2026-33017, came under active exploitation within 20 hours of public disclosure. The vulnerability involves missing authentication combined with code injection, allowing attackers to achieve remote code execution on affected systems.

This rapid weaponization highlights the speed at which threat actors monitor and abuse newly published vulnerabilities in popular AI development tools. Organizations using Langflow must apply patches immediately to prevent unauthorized access and potential data exfiltration.

breaking_news.title: Russian APT28 Exploits Zimbra Vulnerability Against Ukrainian Government
breaking_news.summary:
Russian military intelligence group APT28 is exploiting a critical XSS vulnerability in Zimbra Collaboration Suite to target Ukrainian government entities. The attack leverages CVE-2025-66376 to run scripts via HTML emails, potentially allowing attackers to take over user accounts and compromise the environment.

CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering federal agencies to patch the issue urgently. This campaign underscores the ongoing use of email-based vectors by state-sponsored actors to gain initial access in geopolitical conflicts.
